description: Remediation of issues found in AUDIT.md — fix bugs, improve quality, and raise the count/stability of working proxies (esp. for Russia). Stack: Python orchestrator (main.py, merge.py, core/*) + Go checking core (go_core/main.go) + GitHub Actions CI. Quality checks: Python `python -m py_compile <files>` (and `pytest -q` once tests exist); Go `cd go_core && go build -o /dev/null ./...` and `go vet ./...`. There is no typescript/typecheck in this repo.

userStories.title: Speed as ranking signal, not a hard filter
userStories.description: As a user in Russia, I want more working proxies in the output, so transient bandwidth contention on the CI runner does not drop otherwise-alive nodes.
acceptanceCriteria: In go_core/main.go a node that passes the L7 connectivity ping (HTTP 204) survives even when measured speed is below min_speed
Speed is still measured and stored on the node (used only for sorting/labels, not for dropping)
min_speed becomes an opt-in ranking threshold via a settings flag (e.g. speed_as_filter, default false) instead of an unconditional cut in testHTTPSpeed
Speed-test concurrency (semSpeed) is reduced or made configurable to lower channel contention
`cd go_core && go build -o /dev/null ./...` succeeds and `go vet ./...` is clean
Rationale appended to progress.txt
userStories.notes: AUDIT.md §2.4 — biggest immediate win on working-proxy count.

userStories.title: Remove dead code and extract shared UUID util
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want no duplicated/dead code so the codebase is easier to reason about.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove the unused Inspector.champion_run method in core/engine.py (champion phase lives in Go)
Create core/util.py exposing is_valid_uuid(val) -> bool
core/parser.py and core/engine.py import is_valid_uuid from core.util instead of redefining it
`python -m py_compile main.py merge.py core/*.py` passes
`python -c "import core.parser, core.engine, core.util"` runs without error

userStories.title: Add .gitignore for build/runtime artifacts
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want generated artifacts excluded from git so they cannot be committed by accident.
acceptanceCriteria: Root .gitignore ignores: data/, go_core/angra_core, go_core/angra_core.exe, *.log, __pycache__/, *.pyc, .last-branch, archive/
`git status --porcelain` shows none of those paths as untracked after a dry run that creates them
Already-tracked source files remain tracked (no source accidentally ignored)

userStories.title: Parse once: NODES_FILE input mode + round-robin sharding
userStories.description: As an operator, I want parsing to run once instead of 4x (once per drone), so CI does less redundant work and sharding is balanced.
acceptanceCriteria: Add parse_only.py that runs RKNValidator.load_lists() + LinkParser.fetch_and_parse() and writes data/nodes.json (list of model_dump(by_alias=True) dicts)
main.py reads NODES_FILE env var: when set, it loads nodes from that JSON and skips fetch_and_parse()
Sharding uses round-robin (all_nodes[shard_index::shard_count]) instead of contiguous slicing
When NODES_FILE is unset, behavior is unchanged (backwards compatible)
`python -m py_compile parse_only.py main.py` passes

userStories.title: GeoIP efficiency: dedup hosts and /24 country cache
userStories.description: As an operator, I want GeoIP resolution to avoid duplicate work and reduce ip-api rate-limit risk.
acceptanceCriteria: core/engine.py _resolve_geo deduplicates hosts before DNS resolution (resolve each unique host once)
Country cache is keyed by IPv4 /24 network (fallback to exact IP for IPv6)
Number of ip-api lookups for a fixed node set is provably reduced (logged before/after counts)
`python -c "import core.engine"` runs without error

userStories.title: Rolling pool of historically-working nodes
userStories.description: As a user, I want the working-proxy list to stay populated even when upstream sources are temporarily down or pruned.
acceptanceCriteria: merge.py persists surviving nodes to data/pool.json with fields: uri, last_seen (ISO), fail_count
Node set at run start is the union of freshly parsed nodes and pooled nodes (pooled nodes get re-checked)
A pooled node is evicted after 3 consecutive runs where it failed checks; fail_count resets to 0 on success
pool.json is created on first run if absent and never grows unbounded (evicted nodes removed)
`python -m py_compile merge.py` passes
userStories.notes: AUDIT.md §4.1 — biggest win on stability/count over time.

userStories.title: Publish output to a dedicated branch (no force-push on main)
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want published subscriptions to stop rewriting main history with amend+force.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/update.yml publishes sub_all.txt, sub_bs.txt, sub_chs.txt and index.html to a dedicated branch (e.g. gh-pages) using a normal commit
No `git commit --amend` and no `git push --force` against main
vercel.json / deployment notes updated to serve subscriptions from the publish branch
Workflow YAML is valid (parses with `python -c "import yaml,sys; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/update.yml'))"`)

userStories.title: Parser tests and linters in CI
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want regression protection on the most fragile component (the link parser) and automated lint.
acceptanceCriteria: Add tests/test_parser.py with table-driven cases covering vless/vmess/ss/trojan/hy2 — valid links produce expected ProxyConfig fields, garbage links return None
Add requirements-dev.txt with pytest (and ruff)
`pytest -q` passes locally
A CI workflow (e.g. .github/workflows/ci.yml) runs ruff, pytest, and `cd go_core && go vet ./...` on push/PR
All new code passes `python -m py_compile`

userStories.title: Cache whitelist lists and log list age
userStories.description: As an operator, I want BS/whitelist classification to survive a temporary outage of the upstream whitelist sources.
acceptanceCriteria: RKNValidator caches the last successfully fetched whitelist to data/whitelist_cache/ (domains + cidr)
When a fetch fails or returns empty, the cached copy is loaded instead of leaving lists empty
load_lists logs the source counts and a warning when falling back to cache or when lists are empty
`python -c "import core.validator"` runs without error
userStories.notes: AUDIT.md §5.1.3 — keeps the Nightbird (BS) class from collapsing to zero.

userStories.title: RU-verified subscription scaffolding
userStories.description: As a user in Russia, I want a subscription verified from inside RU; this story adds the plumbing so an RU probe can publish a separate list without an external VPS being required for the code to be correct.
acceptanceCriteria: Exporter can emit an additional sub_ru.txt built from nodes flagged ru_verified=True on the ProxyNode model
A documented, env-gated ingestion path (e.g. RU_VERDICT_FILE) lets an external RU worker submit a list of node ids that passed from RU; absent the file, sub_ru.txt is simply empty (no failure)
vercel.json exposes /sub/ru -> sub_ru.txt
README/AUDIT updated with how to run an RU probe worker
`python -m py_compile core/exporter.py core/models.py` passes
userStories.notes: AUDIT.md §5.1 — the single biggest lever for real-world RU effectiveness. Code-only scaffolding here; the actual RU worker is operational, not part of this repo's CI.
